steps.title: Find the defining contrast
steps.content: France and Japan were already at 70%+ in 1970, so they cannot account for post-1970 urbanization growth. The passage identifies Algeria as a main contributor to world urbanization since 1970, which means Algeria must have started well below 70% in 1970 and risen substantially by 2020.

steps.title: Match the pattern to the graph data
steps.content: The graph shows Algeria at approximately 40% urban in 1970 and roughly 70% in 2020—a dramatic ~30 percentage point increase that explains Algeria's role as a main contributor. Choice D states “around 40% urban in 1970 to more than 70% urban in 2020,” which matches this growth pattern exactly. Choices A and B overstate 2020 (claiming around 90% instead of ~70%), and C underestimates both values. That matches choice D — D is correct.
